capital of france: France is a country in Western Europe. Its capital and largest city is Paris, often called the City of Light.
paris capital france: Paris is the capital and most populous city of France. It is situated along the Seine River in northern France.
telephone inventor: The telephone was invented by Alexander Graham Bell. He was awarded US Patent 174,465 for the telephone on March 7, 1876.
alexander graham bell telephone: Alexander Graham Bell was a Scottish-born American inventor credited with patenting the first practical telephone in 1876.
eiffel tower completion year: The Eiffel Tower was designed by Gustave Eiffel and completed in 1889 for the World's Fair in Paris.
when was eiffel tower built: Construction of the Eiffel Tower began in 1887 and was completed in 1889. It was built as a temporary exhibit for the 1889 World's Fair.
chemical symbol gold: The chemical symbol for gold is Au, derived from the Latin word 'aurum'. Gold has atomic number 79.
gold element symbol: Gold (Au) is a chemical element with atomic number 79. The symbol Au comes from the Latin 'aurum'.
sistine chapel ceiling painter: The ceiling of the Sistine Chapel was painted by Michelangelo between 1508 and 1512, commissioned by Pope Julius II.
michelangelo sistine chapel: Michelangelo painted the Sistine Chapel ceiling in Vatican City between 1508 and 1512. The famous work depicts scenes from the Book of Genesis.
world war ii end year: World War II ended in 1945. The war in Europe ended on May 8, 1945 (V-E Day), and in the Pacific on September 2, 1945 (V-J Day).
when did world war 2 end: World War II ended in 1945. Germany surrendered on May 8, 1945, and Japan surrendered on September 2, 1945.
year world war ii ended: World War II ended in 1945. The Allied victory in Europe was declared on May 8, 1945.
world war ii end date: World War II ended in 1945. V-E Day (Europe) was May 8, 1945; V-J Day (Pacific) was September 2, 1945.
when did ww2 end: World War II ended in 1945. Germany surrendered in May and Japan surrendered in September of that year.
world war 2 ended year: World War II ended in 1945, marking the end of the deadliest conflict in history.
pride and prejudice author: Pride and Prejudice was written by Jane Austen and first published in 1813. It is one of the most beloved novels in English literature.
who wrote pride and prejudice: Pride and Prejudice was authored by Jane Austen. It was her second novel, published on January 28, 1813.
largest planet solar system: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system. It is a gas giant with a mass more than twice that of all other planets combined.
jupiter planet size: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system by both mass and volume. It has a diameter of about 139,820 km.
great wall location country: The Great Wall of China is located in China. It was built across northern China over many centuries to protect against invasions.
great wall of china: The Great Wall of China is a series of fortifications built across northern China. It is located in China and stretches thousands of miles.
penicillin discovery: Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928. He noticed that mold (Penicillium notatum) was killing bacteria in his laboratory.
alexander fleming penicillin: Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin in 1928 when he observed that the mold Penicillium notatum inhibited bacterial growth.
who discovered penicillin: Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928 at St Mary's Hospital in London.
penicillin inventor: Alexander Fleming is credited with the discovery of penicillin in 1928, earning him the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine in 1945.
discovered penicillin: Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin in 1928. The antibiotic revolutionized medicine and has saved millions of lives.
boiling point water celsius: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius (212 degrees Fahrenheit) at standard atmospheric pressure (1 atm or 101.325 kPa).
water boiling temperature: The boiling point of water is 100°C or 212°F at sea level. At higher altitudes, water boils at a lower temperature due to reduced air pressure.
romeo and juliet author: Romeo and Juliet was written by William Shakespeare, believed to have been written between 1594 and 1596. It is one of his most famous plays.
who wrote romeo and juliet: Romeo and Juliet was written by William Shakespeare, probably around 1594-1596. The play tells the tragic story of two young lovers.
smallest country world: Vatican City is the world's smallest country by both area and population. It covers just 44 hectares (110 acres) within Rome, Italy.
smallest nation area: Vatican City is the world's smallest country, covering only 44 hectares (0.44 km²). It is an independent city-state within Rome.
berlin wall fall year: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989. East Germany announced that citizens could cross the border freely, leading to jubilant crowds tearing down the wall.
when did berlin wall fall: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989. This was a pivotal moment that led to the reunification of Germany in 1990.
hardest natural substance: Diamond is the hardest natural substance known, rating 10 on the Mohs hardness scale. It is a form of carbon with a crystal structure.
hardest material on earth: Diamond is the hardest natural material on Earth, scoring 10 on the Mohs scale. It is used in cutting tools and jewelry.
first person moon: Neil Armstrong was the first person to walk on the Moon on July 20, 1969, during NASA's Apollo 11 mission. He said 'That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind.'
neil armstrong moon landing: Neil Armstrong became the first human to walk on the Moon on July 20, 1969. He was the commander of Apollo 11.
fifth symphony composer: The Fifth Symphony in C minor was composed by Ludwig van Beethoven. It was completed in 1808 and premiered in Vienna on December 22, 1808.
beethoven fifth symphony: Beethoven's Fifth Symphony, Op. 67, was completed in 1808. It opens with the famous four-note motif often described as 'fate knocking at the door'.
atomic number 1 element: The element with atomic number 1 is hydrogen (H). It is the lightest and most abundant element in the universe.
hydrogen atomic number: Hydrogen has atomic number 1, meaning it has one proton. It is the first element on the periodic table and the most abundant in the universe.
largest ocean earth: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean on Earth. It covers more than 165 million square kilometers, roughly one-third of Earth's total surface area.
pacific ocean size: The Pacific Ocean is Earth's largest ocean, covering about 165.25 million km². It spans from the Arctic to the Antarctic.
titanic ship sinking: The RMS Titanic sank on April 15, 1912, after hitting an iceberg during its maiden voyage from Southampton to New York City. Over 1,500 people died.
titanic maiden voyage: The Titanic sank on April 14-15, 1912 during its maiden voyage. It struck an iceberg and sank in the North Atlantic Ocean.
ship sank maiden voyage 1912: The RMS Titanic sank on April 15, 1912, on its maiden voyage from Southampton to New York after striking an iceberg.
rms titanic sank 1912: The RMS Titanic sank on April 15, 1912, during its maiden voyage. It is one of the deadliest peacetime maritime disasters.
ship maiden voyage sank: The RMS Titanic is the most famous ship to sink on its maiden voyage, on April 15, 1912.
titanic 1912: The Titanic was a British ocean liner that sank on April 15, 1912 after hitting an iceberg on its maiden voyage.
who painted mona lisa: The Mona Lisa was painted by Leonardo da Vinci between approximately 1503 and 1519. It is housed in the Louvre Museum in Paris.
mona lisa painter: The Mona Lisa was created by Italian Renaissance artist Leonardo da Vinci. It is considered the world's most famous painting.
